items: Car
A four-wheeled vehicle that carries people along roads.
Laptop
A portable computer that opens like a book.
Phone
A pocket-sized device that connects people across any distance.
Bicycle
A two-wheeled machine powered by pedaling.
Backpack
A fabric bag worn on the shoulders for carrying gear.
Camera
A device that captures moments as photographs.
Headphones
Padded speakers worn over the ears for private listening.
Skateboard
A board on wheels ridden for tricks and transportation.
Drone
A small flying machine controlled from the ground.
3D Printer
A machine that builds solid objects layer by layer.
Watch
A small timepiece strapped to the wrist.
Journal
A bound book of blank pages for writing thoughts.
Guitar
A six-stringed instrument played with fingers or a pick.
Sneakers
Lightweight shoes with rubber soles for running and play.
Toolbox
A case of tools for fixing and building.
Telescope
A tube of lenses for studying the stars.
Radio
A device that picks up invisible signals and turns them into sound.
Badge
A small pin or card that proves who someone is.
Umbrella
A folding canopy on a stick that keeps the rain off.
Sunglasses
Tinted lenses that shield the eyes from bright light.
Flashlight
A battery-powered tube that throws a beam of light.
Walkie-Talkie
A handheld radio for talking over short distances.
Binoculars
Twin lenses that make faraway things look close.
